_comment: Evergreen day 4 — Notwithstanding clause. Constitutional explainer.
matchSubstring: The notwithstanding clause, in plain English
text:
The notwithstanding clause, in plain English:

Section 33 of the Charter lets Parliament or a province override sections 2 and 7-15 for 5 years at a time.

It cannot override voting rights, mobility, language, or Indigenous rights.

Federal Parliament has never used it.
